key_outputs.clusters: 5 clusters: (1) Sparse/Differentiable Attention [HIGH], (2) Rationale-Supervised Attention [HIGH], (3) Explainable Hate Speech [HIGH], (4) Faithfulness Evaluation [HIGH], (5) Fairness/Target Bias [MEDIUM]
key_outputs.top_gaps: Gap1: No sparsemax+rationale hate speech paper exists; Gap2: Hard sparsity vs classification trade-off unknown; Gap3: Cross-dataset generalization untested
key_outputs.tier1_papers: eilertsen2025aligning, mathew2021hatexplain, martins2016sparsemax, correia2019adaptively, jain2019attention, wiegreffe2019attention, deyoung2020eraser, gao2025rise

summary: Pass 1 complete. 54 papers, 5 clusters, 3 gaps. Primary competitor: eilertsen2025aligning (SRA, AAAI 2026) uses softmax. Our sparsemax+rationale gap is confirmed unoccupied.
critical_context: eilertsen2025aligning (SRA, AAAI 2026): softmax+KL loss on HateXplain; our distinction is structural zero-attention via sparsemax
mathew2021hatexplain: 20K posts, ~25% token density — well-suited for sparse attention
martins2016sparsemax: differentiable, exact-zero weights, efficient Jacobian — technically feasible
ERASER metrics: comprehensiveness+sufficiency are primary faithfulness evaluation
Mini-project: significant comprehensiveness gain; sufficiency inconsistent (3 seeds)
